content: Just as the language learning definition of reflexion frames it as both reflecting incoming input *and* internalizing that input to improve one’s own output, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to explicitly integrate this dual function rather than only focusing on critiquing past outputs. This creates a new feedback loop where LLMs first "reflect" ambient external human preference data like a mirror before turning that reflection inward to revise their generation standards, instead of only self-critiquing existing outputs.
